Sonny Styles.source: 2026 Combine — LB record 43.5" vert, 4.46 forty. Historic athlete.

Dillon Thieneman.source: 2026 Combine — 4.35 forty (4th fastest S), 41" vert, 1.52 10-yd split. Locked in 1st round.

Kenyon Sadiq.source: 2026 Combine — TE record 4.39 forty, 43.5" vert, 11'1" broad. Clear TE1.

Malachi Fields.source: 2026 Combine — 4.61 forty + concentration drops in on-field drills. Stock down.

Omar Cooper Jr..source: 2026 Combine — 4.42 forty. Daniel Jeremiah bumped to No. 19 on updated big board.

Rueben Bain Jr..source: 2026 Combine — 30 7/8" arms (4th shortest among edge rushers since 1999) raised ceiling concerns.

Eli Stowers.source: 2026 Combine — TE records: 45.5" vertical and 11'3" broad jump. Both all-time records for TEs.

Mike Washington Jr..source: 2026 Combine — 4.33 forty (fastest RB) at 223 lbs. Stock up to Day 2 consideration.

Deion Burks.source: 2026 Combine — 4.30 forty, 42.5" vert, 10'11" broad. 3rd fastest overall at combine.

Zachariah Branch.source: 2026 Combine — 4.35 forty. NFL.com stock up to top-40 consideration.

Fernando Mendoza.source: UNANIMOUS #1 — only consensus pick across all six expert mocks (Kiper, Jeremiah, Brugler, Yates, Ringer, B/R). Locked in to Raiders at #1.

Arvell Reese.source: Final mocks — consensus #2 to Jets (Brugler, B/R, Ringer, Jeremiah). Top EDGE on most boards with 4.46 + elite 10-yd split.

David Bailey.source: Final mocks — Kiper & Yates have him #2 to Jets. Consensus #3.7 ADP. Elite pro day rush drills.

Carnell Tate.source: Pro day — 4.45 forty answered combine concerns (was 4.53 at combine, Beane called him "slow"). Back into consensus top-6 after Cleveland visit. Browns mulling at #6.

Jeremiyah Love.source: Lone consensus R1 RB. ESPN reports Cardinals showing strong interest at #3; alternative landing spot Giants #5.

Jordyn Tyson.source: Stock rising into top-12. Yates & Kiper both mock to Cowboys at #12. "Best route runner in the class."

Jermod McCoy.source: Only consensus R1 CB. Cowboys, Rams, Ravens all in top-15. "Top-15 pick thanks to thin CB class."

Kadyn Proctor.source: Into consensus top-15. B/R mocks to Browns #6; Kiper #12. Massive frame + feet have him as top blocker for some evaluators.

Monroe Freeling.source: Four of six expert mocks slot him to Lions #17 — natural replacement for Taylor Decker.

Emmanuel McNeil-Warren.source: Bounced back from combine struggles in pro day workouts (was 4.52 at combine). All six expert mocks now have him in R1 (ADP 24.5). Eagles the frontrunner.

Makai Lemon.source: Consensus #14 (Ravens). Crisp route running + elite hands made him a consistent teens pick across boards.

KC Concepcion.source: Consensus #22 (Chargers). Electric separator with 4.38 speed — fits Harbaugh offense.

Caleb Downs.source: Consensus top-7 lock. Brugler & Yates both mock to Commanders at #7. "Slam dunk top-10 selection."

Mansoor Delane.source: Top CB/DB on most boards. Consensus #10 to Giants. Versatile — projects to outside or slot.

Ty Simpson.source: Into late R1 consensus as #2 QB behind Mendoza. Yates mocks #26 (BUF trade up), Kiper #30 (Cowboys trade up), Ringer late R1.

Max Iheanachor.source: Late riser — 34.5" arms and explosive pro day moved him into consensus R1 (ADP 29.7). Steelers, Dolphins, Cardinals doing homework.

Zion Young.source: Final mocks — Yates #25 (NE), Kiper #25 (NE). First-team All-SEC with massive 2025 leap. 10 pre-draft visits.

Malachi Lawrence.source: Late-R1 sleeper. B/R #32 (Seattle), Brugler #26 (Buffalo). Elite bend + twitch made scouts fans late in the process.

Jadarian Price.source: Squeaked into R1 in Kiper and Jeremiah final mocks (#32). Explosive complement to Love at Notre Dame.
